feature_name: v1.22.1 mobile-player follow-up: targeted bug-fix round on the v1.22.0 FR-1 responsive player. FIVE grounded on-device bugs -- (1) mobile AUDIO missing playback-speed/hold-2x + non-functional fullscreen button; (2) mobile VIDEO shows NO visible controls in FULL [CRITICAL live regression -- neither the hidden custom bar nor the native strip appears, only the double-tap 15s skip works]; (3) rotate-to-landscape does not enter fullscreen (audio + video); (4) desktop custom bar has NO persistent playback-speed control (audio + video); (5) desktop click-on-video does not toggle play/pause. Unifying theme across bugs 1 + 4: playback speed is not a first-class control on the custom bar anywhere. Dean directive: keep this round SUPER quick -- smallest correct fixes over any refactor, minimize tasks/waves, heavy gate ONLY where genuinely regression-prone. Almost all changes confined to public/js/player.js + public/css/style.css (+ maybe the four shells for a speed button).
base: main (v1.22.0). Prior shipped record: docs/exec-plans/completed/ v1.22.0 'Player Parity + Roadmap' (the round that introduced the FR-1 responsive-controls split these four bugs regress/expose).
scope_summary: FOUR bugs from Dean's iOS on-device test of v1.22.0, driven as one lean round. Resist scope creep beyond these four. Coordinator owns ALL git; SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint output and DO NOT commit. Node 22 toolchain at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-coder-projects-filetube/139c0e56-b545-4e8e-ba05-f892f6dd6d0d/scratchpad/node-v22.23.1-linux-x64/bin (prepend to PATH). Tests npm test, lint npm run lint (baseline 0 errors, 8 pre-existing common.js warnings). No headless/E2E -- Dean's iOS on-device pass is the documented arbiter (docs/RELIABILITY.md).

grounded_diagnosis.bug1_mobile_audio: Design per v1.22.0 FR-1: mobile AUDIO keeps the v1.21 custom bar (minus #vol-bar/#mute-btn, hidden by style.css:3218-3220). #fs-btn REMAINS on the custom bar for audio -> Dean taps it, it routes through enterFullscreen() (player.js:1395) which on iOS calls mediaPlayer.webkitEnterFullscreen(); for an AUDIO item there is no video surface so iOS no-ops/refuses -> 'fullscreen button doesn't work'. Fix: either remove #fs-btn for mobile audio (audio has no fullscreen concept -- matches AC11's own intent) or make it meaningful (fullscreen the cover-art) -- PM/PE decide. hold-2x is wired on #audio-bg-art (player.js:1671 wireSkipHoldGestures(audioBgArt,...)) and guarded state!==STATE_FULL; confirm during Design why it does not engage on device (candidate: gesture/tap classification, or the pointer-events swap in .audio-mode). No persistent speed affordance exists on the custom bar at all -> UNIFIES with bug 4.
grounded_diagnosis.bug2_mobile_video_CRITICAL: LEADING HYPOTHESIS (PE must confirm root cause precisely in Design before designing the fix). resolveControlsMode(currentData.type,'video',mobile) returns 'native' and applyControlsMode() sets the native `controls` attribute only while state===STATE_FULL (player.js:520-530), while style.css:3201 hides the custom bar for #player-wrapper.ff-mobile:not(.audio-mode). So on mobile VIDEO in FULL the custom bar is hidden AND the `controls` attribute is present -- yet Dean sees NEITHER. Ruled out: no ::-webkit-media-controls{display:none} suppression exists in style.css (grepped); currentData.type is exactly 'video' for video so the string check is correct; if isMobileFormFactor() returned FALSE the custom bar would SHOW (contradicts 'no controls'), so .ff-mobile IS being set. Strongest remaining root cause: FileTube's own gesture layer (wireSkipHoldGestures on #media-player, player.js:1670, plus the .skip-controls overlay) SWALLOWS the single taps that iOS Safari needs to RE-REVEAL its auto-hidden inline native controls -- iOS inline `<video controls playsinline>` controls auto-hide, and only a tap ON the video re-shows them, but the gesture handler intercepts/handles that tap (single-tap->play/pause, double-tap->skip, hold->2x) so the native strip, once auto-hidden, never returns. This EXACTLY matches 'no visible controls -- if i tap forward/back i can get the 15s skip but that's it' (the .skip-controls overlay still receives the double-tap). Secondary candidate to check: a transparent overlay (.skip-controls) covering the native strip region. The v1.22.0 FR-1 design assumption that 'gestures already coexist with a native control strip the way they did pre-v1.21' appears WRONG for iOS -- PE must root-cause on the actual iOS behavior and pick the approach that ACTUALLY WORKS on iOS (native-with-reveal-tap-passthrough, OR a properly touch-sized custom bar for mobile video too -- whatever gives play/pause + seek + fullscreen + speed on iOS).
grounded_diagnosis.bug3_rotate_fullscreen: An orientationchange->fullscreen listener ALREADY EXISTS (player.js:1685-1711): matchMedia('(orientation: landscape)') change -> enterFullscreen() when state===STATE_FULL and not already fullscreen. It routes through enterFullscreen() -> mediaPlayer.webkitEnterFullscreen() on iOS. LEADING ROOT CAUSE: iOS Safari refuses programmatic fullscreen (webkitEnterFullscreen / requestFullscreen) that is NOT initiated by a direct user gesture; an orientationchange event does NOT count as a user activation on iPhone, so the call is silently refused. This is a genuine iOS PLATFORM LIMITATION -- flag as a SCOPE CALL for Dean: on iPhone Safari, YouTube-app-style auto-rotate-to-fullscreen of an inline element may simply not be permitted; the native fullscreen button (bug-2 fix) is the reliable path. For AUDIO there is no fullscreen concept at all. PE to confirm and scope honestly.
grounded_diagnosis.bug4_desktop_speed: The v1.21 custom #player-controls bar replaced native <audio>/<video> controls but never re-exposed a playback-speed affordance, so DESKTOP lost speed control for BOTH audio and video (native context-menu/speed menu gone with the custom bar). Fix (unifies with bug 1): add a PERSISTENT playback-speed control to the custom bar -- visible & tappable on desktop AND the mobile custom bar, touch-target >=44px on mobile -- cycling 1x/1.25/1.5/1.75/2x (or a small menu; PE's call). A .speed-badge element ALREADY EXISTS for the hold-2x gesture (style.css:1115, player.js speedBadge) -- PE must decide whether the persistent control reuses/extends .speed-badge or is a new #speed-btn, so the two do NOT conflict. On mobile VIDEO: if bug-2's fix routes to native controls, iOS native already provides a speed menu -> confirm whether our speed control is needed there or would double up; do NOT double up. PiP on desktop video is GOOD -- leave it untouched.
grounded_diagnosis.bug5_desktop_click_to_pause: On DESKTOP, clicking the video surface does NOT toggle play/pause. Grounded: #audio-bg-art has a click->toggle listener (player.js:1646, FULL-only, debounced via scheduleArtSingleTap) but #media-player (VIDEO) has wireSkipHoldGestures(mediaPlayer) wired with NO onSingleTap (player.js:1670), so a single click/tap on the video does nothing. Fix: add YouTube-style click-on-video->toggle-play/pause for DESKTOP video. Must (a) isolate from the #player-controls bar and .skip-controls so a control-click or a double-tap-skip does not ALSO toggle play (reuse the existing scheduleArtSingleTap/cancelPendingArtTap debounce the audio path already uses to avoid the double-click flicker vs dblclick-skip); (b) NOT break mobile single-tap semantics (on mobile video, if bug-2 routes to native controls, native owns the tap; the desktop click-to-pause must be scoped so it does not conflict with the mobile gesture path). Small additive change on the video interaction surface -- PE confirms exact scoping.

unification_note: Bugs 1 (mobile-audio speed/hold-2x) and 4 (desktop speed) share ONE root theme: playback speed is not a first-class control on the custom bar anywhere. Design should add ONE persistent speed control (desktop + mobile custom bar, audio + video) and keep the mobile hold-2x gesture as the quick-access shortcut. Do NOT create two divergent speed mechanisms.
proposed_fr_shape_for_pm: Recommend the PM structure the exec plan as 5 grounded FRs (lean, one bug each): FR-1 = bug 2 (mobile-video controls, CRITICAL, HEAVIEST gate + Dean iOS arbiter, root-cause FIRST); FR-2 = bug 1 (mobile-audio fs-btn fix/removal + hold-2x); FR-3 = bug 3 (rotate-to-fullscreen audio+video, with an explicit iOS platform-limit scope call for Dean -- may be a no-code/honest-scope outcome); FR-4 = bug 4 unified with bug-1 speed (ONE persistent playback-speed control on the custom bar, desktop+mobile, audio+video, reconciled with the existing .speed-badge); FR-5 = bug 5 (desktop click-video-to-toggle-play/pause, reusing the audio path's scheduleArtSingleTap debounce). All five almost certainly touch only public/js/player.js + public/css/style.css (+ the four byte-identical shells IF FR-4 adds a #speed-btn to #player-controls). KEEP IT MINIMAL per Dean: smallest correct fix per bug, no refactor of the FR-1 architecture beyond each root cause.
hard_constraints: SMALL, surgical scope: exactly these four bugs. NO broad rework of the v1.22.0 FR-1 architecture beyond what each bug's root cause requires. Resist scope creep.

Additive / no regressions: must preserve every v1.16/v1.21/v1.22.0 player feature (dock FULL/DOCKED/CLOSED, prev/next, autoplay-next, transcode + resume overlays, Media Session, progress saving, audio-bg-art, loop toggle, cross-tab, PiP). The persistent <video id=media-player> single element + one-time wireHostListeners() model is unchanged in kind.

DESKTOP behavior that Dean already likes stays intact EXCEPT the additive speed control (bug 4): desktop video PiP is explicitly good -- leave it. Do not regress desktop audio/video custom-bar behavior otherwise.

The four shells share a byte-identical #player-host-template (public/index.html, public/setup.html, public/watch.html, lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html) -- if any new control id (e.g. #speed-btn) is added to #player-controls it MUST be byte-identical across ALL FOUR shells (parity-test), same discipline as v1.22.0's #pip-btn.

Preserve the era-theme token system for all new/changed CSS (speed control styling, any mobile-video control fix, touch-target sizing) -- no hardcoded colors bypassing the tokens; >=44px touch targets on mobile per the existing convention.

Follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes, textContent over innerHTML, node:test for every new pure helper + regression lock, lint 0 warnings, NO new runtime dependencies.
Reuse the SINGLE shared mobile-detection helper (resolveMobileFormFactor/isMobileFormFactor) -- never introduce a second divergent 'is this mobile' signal.

Node.js 22 LTS toolchain; no headless-browser/E2E infra (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- Dean's iOS on-device pass is the documented arbiter for every mobile-feel item here (bugs 1/2/3) and the desktop speed control feel (bug 4).
ios_scope_flags_for_dean: Bug 3 (rotate-to-fullscreen): iOS Safari likely refuses programmatic fullscreen without a direct user gesture -- auto-rotate-to-fullscreen of an inline element may be impossible on iPhone. If PE confirms, this is scoped honestly (native fullscreen BUTTON is the reliable path), not silently 'fixed'.

Bug 2 (mobile-video controls): the ACTUAL iOS behavior (native inline controls auto-hide + gesture-layer tap-swallow) drives whether the fix is native-with-passthrough or a touch-sized custom bar -- PE picks whatever demonstrably works on iOS, which Dean's on-device pass arbitrates.
Bug 1 (mobile-audio fullscreen button): audio has no native fullscreen concept on iOS -- the honest fix is likely REMOVING #fs-btn for mobile audio rather than making it work.
review_tiers_proposed: Per Dean's 'keep it super quick' directive -- heavy gate ONLY where genuinely regression-prone. HEAVIEST two-reviewer/adversarial + Dean iOS arbiter for FR-1 (bug 2, CRITICAL persistent-player mount/dock controls path) and for ANYTHING touching the persistent-player mount/dock/applyControlsMode path. LIGHT single-QA + Dean on-device for the smaller additive bits: FR-2 (bug 1), FR-3 (bug 3 rotate listener), FR-4 (speed control -- light UNLESS its shell/#player-controls touch is judged higher-risk by PE, in which case a parity-test on the four shells suffices), FR-5 (click-to-pause). Dean's on-device pass (iOS + desktop) is the ultimate arbiter across the board.
dean_speed_directive: Keep the ENTIRE round minimal and fast. Prefer the smallest correct fix over any refactor. Minimize the number of tasks/waves. Anticipated task shape: just T1 (public/js/player.js -- all five bugs' JS logic + new pure helpers/tests) and T2 (public/css/style.css -- speed-button styling, touch targets, mobile-video control-visibility/overlay fix; T2 written against T1's frozen .ff-mobile/#speed-btn contract), + a small addition to the four shells folded into T1 only IF FR-4 needs a #speed-btn in #player-host-template. player.js and style.css are disjoint files so T1||T2 can run in one parallel wave with a frozen contract (mirrors v1.22.0's T1/T2 split). EM to finalize the exact task/wave plan after PE Design.

pe_design.fr1_rootcause_verdict: Architectural: v1.22.0 made the iOS native control strip the SOLE mobile-video control surface with NO fallback (applyControlsMode sets `controls` only in FULL; style.css:3201 hides the custom bar for .ff-mobile:not(.audio-mode)) -- so if the native strip fails to present, the user has nothing. The coordinator's 'skip-overlay eats the reveal tap' hypothesis is PARTIALLY REFUTED by CSS: .skip-controls is pointer-events:none with only small left/right pill buttons (12%) active for 2.5s, so the video CENTER stays tap-through and a lone tap's touchend does not preventDefault -- a center tap reaches #media-player. The real, un-fixable-from-JS factor is iOS Safari's internal auto-hide/re-reveal of inline <video controls playsinline> controls (shadow-DOM behavior a page cannot observe or drive), which under our non-passive touchend + full-surface skip overlay + gesture layer demonstrably does not re-reveal on Dean's device. v1.22.0's 'gestures coexist with native strip like pre-v1.21' was wrong (pre-v1.21 `controls` was hard-coded in markup, always present, no FULL-only JS gating, no hidden-custom-bar fallback).
pe_design.fr1_fix: Direction (B) -- RETIRE the mobile-video native-controls path and route mobile video through the same custom #player-controls bar that already works for desktop + mobile audio. Demonstrably works (bar always rendered, not dependent on iOS shadow-DOM reveal); smaller/lower-risk (deletes special-casing vs adding skip-zone geometry); matches Dean's stated preference (custom-bar parity when achievable -- it is); native still available on-demand+reliably via #fs-btn -> webkitEnterFullscreen (user gesture -> full native iOS fullscreen chrome). AC2 set delivered via custom bar (pp/seek/fs-btn/#speed-btn/existing download btn); AC3 gestures preserved (wireSkipHoldGestures unchanged). player.js: drop the setAttribute('controls') branch in applyControlsMode, remove now-vestigial resolveControlsMode+export (repoint AC5 test). style.css: delete mobile-video bar-hide (3199-3203) + DOCKED re-assert (3205-3212); .pc-btn 36px->44px on mobile; extend volume-hide to mobile video + add mobile-audio #fs-btn hide.
pe_design.fr3_ios_verdict: PLATFORM LIMIT -- auto-rotate-to-fullscreen is NOT achievable on iPhone Safari (programmatic fullscreen refused without a direct user gesture; orientationchange/matchMedia is not user activation). Do NOT ship fake auto-rotate. Accepted resolution: the reliable MANUAL fullscreen button restored by FR-1 (mobile-video custom bar #fs-btn -> webkitEnterFullscreen). CODE CHANGE: NONE -- existing orientationchange listener (player.js:1685-1711) is already state-guarded + try/catch + promise-catch, cannot crash/loop (AC16 met as-is), and still works harmlessly on desktop/Android; removing it is pure churn. NEEDS DEAN'S EXPLICIT SIGN-OFF (AC15).
pe_design.fr4_mechanism: New #speed-btn CYCLE button on #player-controls (1x->1.25->1.5->1.75->2x->1x), NOT an extension of the transient pointer-events:none .speed-badge hold-2x indicator (kept separate; hold-2x reads/restores live playbackRate so base rate set by #speed-btn is preserved). Cycle over menu = smallest fix (no popup/positioning/fullscreen-layer work). Pure helper nextPlaybackRate(current) unit-tested. localStorage['ft-rate'] persistence (recommended, mirrors ft-loop/ft-volume). Covers ALL cases incl. mobile video (FR-1 routes it to custom bar) -> no double-up (AC21, no native inline menu to collide). NEW id -> byte-identical across the four shells (index/setup/watch/subscriptions), #pip-btn discipline; placed before #fs-btn; folded into T1.
pe_design.fr2_note: #fs-btn hidden via CSS for mobile audio (not markup removal -> no 4-shell churn, no fsBtn ref breakage); resolves the dangling v1.22.0 AC11 fork. hold-2x: leading concrete hypothesis = touchmove clears holdTimer at MOVE_TOL=10px before HOLD_MS=500ms (thumb jitter on cover art); fix = loosen hold-cancel tolerance (~16px). HYPOTHESIS -- Dean's on-device pass is arbiter; change stays tiny/localized.
pe_design.fr5_note: Desktop-only click listener on #media-player mirroring the #audio-bg-art handler, reusing scheduleArtSingleTap/cancelPendingArtTap; guarded by state===FULL and isMobileFormFactor() (desktop-only, AC29, reuses shared helper); uses plain togglePlayPause (not toggleArtPlayPause). No control-bar double-fire (AC27) -- #player-controls is a SIBLING of #media-player, clicks don't bubble. dblclick-skip preserved via the pending-timer cancel (AC28).
pe_design.task_plan: One wave, T1||T2 (mirrors v1.22.0). T1 = public/js/player.js + the four shells (#speed-btn) + unit tests (all five FRs' JS; FR-3 no-code). T2 = public/css/style.css (against T1's frozen .ff-mobile/#speed-btn contract). Disjoint files. Review: HEAVIEST two-reviewer + Dean iOS for FR-1; LIGHT single-QA + on-device for FR-2/3/4/5, with the four-shell parity test as FR-4's gating check.

history.note: ENGINEERING-MANAGER opened v1.22.1 'mobile-player follow-up' -- a targeted, lean bug-fix round off main (v1.22.0) for FIVE grounded on-device bugs Dean found in the v1.22.0 FR-1 responsive player (bug 5 + the 'keep it super quick' directive arrived mid-Discovery and were folded in). Grounded the diagnosis directly in the live code before routing: read public/js/player.js (resolveMobileFormFactor/isMobileFormFactor/resolveControlsMode/applyControlsMode:333-530, enterFullscreen:1395, the four 'ended' listeners + loop:1416-1465, mountInSlot/dock/close/load state transitions:1873-1988, the orientationchange->fullscreen listener:1685-1711, the audio click-to-toggle:1646, wireSkipHoldGestures wiring:1670-1671 [note: #media-player wired with NO onSingleTap -> grounds bug 5], keyboard shortcuts:1716-1749) and public/css/style.css (the v1.22 FR-1 responsive-controls section:3176-3237 incl. the mobile-video custom-bar hide:3201, the #player-dock re-assert:3210, the mobile-audio vol/mute drop:3218, the PiP section:3223; confirmed NO ::-webkit-media-controls suppression exists anywhere). Diagnosed each bug (see grounded_diagnosis): bug 1 mobile-audio fs-btn no-op + missing speed; bug 2 [CRITICAL] mobile-video NO visible controls -- leading root cause is the gesture layer swallowing the single taps iOS needs to re-reveal auto-hidden inline native controls (custom bar hidden by CSS, native strip perpetually hidden) -- PE must confirm precisely in Design; bug 3 rotate-to-fullscreen listener ALREADY exists but iOS refuses non-user-gesture fullscreen (platform-limit scope call for Dean); bug 4 desktop custom bar has no persistent speed control (unifies with bug 1 -- ONE speed control theme); bug 5 desktop click-video-to-pause missing (#media-player has no onSingleTap). Set stage=discovery, wrote the product-manager Discovery inbox with the full grounded brief + Dean's minimal-scope/lean-gate directive. No application/test code written; no git touched. Coordinator informed of the plan shape (5 FRs, likely just T1 player.js || T2 style.css in one wave, heavy gate only on bug 2 / mount-dock path) and that the PM inbox is ready.

PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Discovery. Confirmed the EM's grounded diagnosis directly against the live code (player.js lines 330-530/820-915/1380-1420/1640-1720, style.css 1107-1125/3176-3237, watch.html's #player-controls markup) and the shipped v1.22.0 FR-1 design -- no corrections to the original grounding needed. Narrowed FR-1's leading hypothesis with a concrete finding: wireSkipHoldGestures's touchend handler does NOT call preventDefault() on a genuine lone tap for #media-player (only a classified double-tap-skip does) -- so 'the gesture layer swallows every tap' is not the full story; flagged explicitly for PE to root-cause the exact iOS mechanism on real device behavior before designing FR-1's fix (Design's #1 job), rather than assume. Authored docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-07-v1.22.1-mobile-player-fixes.md: 5 lean FRs (one bug each) -- FR-1 bug2/CRITICAL (outcome-focused ACs, mechanism deliberately left to PE), FR-2 bug1 (mobile-audio fs-btn + hold-2x; speed access deferred to FR-4), FR-3 bug3 (rotate-to-fullscreen, explicit iOS platform-limit scope call surfaced for Dean's sign-off -- accepts either a working auto-rotate or a documented platform-limit + reliable manual-fullscreen fallback), FR-4 bug4 unified with bug-1's speed gap (ONE persistent playback-speed control, desktop+mobile, reconciled with the existing transient #speed-badge hold-2x indicator, not duplicated onto native mobile-video controls), FR-5 bug5 (desktop click-video-to-pause, reusing the audio path's scheduleArtSingleTap/cancelPendingArtTap debounce). 37 acceptance criteria total, grouped/tagged per FR + cross-cutting, with review tiers (HEAVIEST two-reviewer+Dean-iOS-arbiter for FR-1 only; LIGHT single-QA+on-device for FR-2/3/4/5) per Dean's lean-gate directive. One non-blocking product-fork recommendation: remove (not repurpose) #fs-btn for mobile audio, which also resolves a dangling fork the v1.22.0 FR-1 design left open (AC11 wording vs. shipped behavior). No overlap found with any active exec plan (none exists) or the tech-debt tracker. Set stage=design, wrote artifacts.requirements/exec_plan. No application/test code written; no git touched. Routing back to EM for /prep-pe-design.
